summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Control (in experiments)'?

"Control (in experiments)" means: Holding all variables constant across treatment groups except the variable being tested, so differences can be attributed to the treatment. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Extraneous variable'?

"Extraneous variable" means: Any variable other than the explanatory variable that might affect the response and should be controlled in the experiment. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standardized conditions'?

"Standardized conditions" means: Keeping environmental and procedural factors identical across all treatment groups to prevent them from influencing results. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Replication'?

"Replication" means: Repeating the experiment on many subjects to increase the reliability of results and enable estimation of natural variation. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Statistical control'?

"Statistical control" means: Using statistical techniques (stratification, regression) to adjust for confounding variables when physical control is not possible. The other options describe different concepts.
